popularStory.headline: William Long Legs
popularStory.description:
Sir William of Douglas, known as "Longleg", was born c1200. Why or how he got the name 'Longleg' is unknown to me.

He sided with the English party in the embittered struggle for the control of Scotti …

zh.Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland: 蘇格蘭藍納可爾道格拉斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當William "Longlegs" Douglas 3rd Lord of Douglas於1220年出生在蘇格蘭藍納可爾道格拉斯 卡素時，他的父親Archibald Douglas 2nd Laird Of Douglas42歲，母親Lady Margaret Crawford33歲。他於1243年之前與Constance Battaille of Fawdon在蘇格蘭藍納可爾道格拉斯結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1274年在他的家鄉享年54歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭素斯 藍納可爾道格拉斯聖 布利德也斯 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William "Longlegs" Douglas 3rd Lord of Douglas于1220年出生在苏格兰藍納可爾道格拉斯 卡素时，他的父亲Archibald Douglas 2nd Laird Of Douglas42岁，母亲Lady Margaret Crawford33岁。他于1243年之前与Constance Battaille of Fawdon在苏格兰藍納可爾道格拉斯结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1274年在他的家乡享年54岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰素斯 藍納可爾道格拉斯聖 布利德也斯 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Douglas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called from their situation on a river named with Gaelic dubh ‘dark, black’ + glas ‘stream’ (a derivative of glas ‘blue’). There are several localities in Scotland and Ireland so named, but the one from which the surname is derived in most if not all cases is Douglas in Lanarkshire 20 miles south of Glasgow, the original stronghold of the influential Douglas family and their retainers.

History: The family taking their name from Douglas in Lanarkshire were of Flemish origin. They rose to great prominence in the 14th and 15th centuries, controlling the earldoms of Douglas, Morton, and Angus, and later, Queensberry.

variants.name: Douglass
meanings: Scottish and English (Durham and Northumbria): variant of   Douglas  .

History: William Douglass, a physician recognized for his identification and description of an epidemic of scarlet fever, was born c. 1691 in Gifford, Haddington County, Scotland, and settled in Boston in 1718. The abolitionist, orator, and journalist Frederick Douglass assumed the name after escaping from slavery in 1838 and traveling to Massachusetts. Son of a white father and a slave with some Indian blood, he was born Frederick Augustus Washington Bailey c. 1817 in Tuckahoe, MD.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William "Longlegs" Douglas 3rd Lord of Douglas was born in 1220, in Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Scotland, his father, Archibald Douglas 2nd Laird Of Douglas, was 42 and his mother, Lady Margaret Crawford, was 33. He married Constance Battaille of Fawdon before 1243, in Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1274, in his hometown, at the age of 54, and was buried in Saint Bride's Cemetery, Douglas, South Lanarkshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William "Longlegs" Douglas 3rd Lord of Douglas est né en 1220, à Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Écosse, son père, Archibald Douglas 2nd Laird Of Douglas, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret Crawford, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Constance Battaille of Fawdon avant 1243, à Douglas, Lanarkshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1274, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Saint Bride's Cemetery, Douglas, South Lanarkshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als William "Longlegs" Douglas 3rd Lord of Douglas 1220 in Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Archibald Douglas 2nd Laird Of Douglas 42 und seine Mutter Lady Margaret Crawford 33. Er hat vor 1243 in Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland Constance Battaille of Fawdon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1274 im Alter von 54 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Saint Bride's Cemetery, Douglas, South Lanarkshire, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Constance Battaille of Fawdon prima del 1243, nel Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. ko.Saint Bride's Cemetery, Douglas, South L: 영국 스코틀랜드 소우스 랜아르크실르 더글라스 세인트 브라이데'스 세멧리
ko.lifeSummary: William "Longlegs" Douglas 3rd Lord of Douglas(이)가 1220년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 더글라스 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Archibald Douglas 2nd Laird Of Douglas(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Margaret Crawford(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1243년 이전 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 더글라스에서 Constance Battaille of Fawdon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1274년 그의 고향에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 소우스 랜아르크실르 더글라스 세인트 브라이데'스 세멧리에 묻혔다.

ru.Saint Bride's Cemetery, Douglas, South L: сеинт бриде'с кеметири, доуглас, соут ланаркшир, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда William "Longlegs" Douglas 3rd Lord of Douglas родился в 1220 г. в местности доуглас кастл, ланаркшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Archibald Douglas 2nd Laird Of Douglas было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret Crawford, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Constance Battaille of Fawdon перед 1243 г. в местности доуглас, ланаркшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1274 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности сеинт бриде'с кеметири, доуглас, соут ланаркшир, Шотландия, Великобритания.
